prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
(detect_mission_node recon_unit_alpha)
(assign_path_option_to_node recon_unit_alpha path_option_1)
(attach_intel_to_node recon_unit_alpha intel_fragment_1)
(mark_node_prepared recon_unit_alpha intel_fragment_1)
(attach_equipment_to_node recon_unit_alpha equipment_silencer)
(validate_recon_approach_vector recon_unit_alpha recon_approach_vector_alpha intel_fragment_1)
(finalize_recon_vector_for_recon_unit recon_unit_alpha recon_approach_vector_alpha equipment_silencer)
(release_intel_from_node recon_unit_alpha intel_fragment_1)
(detect_mission_node assault_unit_alpha)
(assign_path_option_to_node assault_unit_alpha path_option_2)
(attach_intel_to_node assault_unit_alpha intel_fragment_1)
(mark_node_prepared assault_unit_alpha intel_fragment_1)
(assign_consumable_to_assault_unit assault_unit_alpha assault_approach_vector_alpha smoke_grenade_1)
(confirm_assault_vector_after_validation assault_unit_alpha assault_approach_vector_alpha intel_fragment_1 smoke_grenade_1)
(release_intel_from_node assault_unit_alpha intel_fragment_1)
(attach_intel_to_node recon_unit_alpha intel_fragment_1)
(assemble_loadout recon_unit_alpha assault_unit_alpha recon_approach_vector_alpha assault_approach_vector_alpha loadout_alpha)
(finalize_loadout loadout_alpha recon_unit_alpha intel_fragment_1)
(mark_recon_unit_ready recon_unit_alpha loadout_alpha)
(mark_assault_unit_ready assault_unit_alpha loadout_alpha)
(detach_equipment_from_node recon_unit_alpha equipment_silencer)
(consume_resource_and_recover_node recon_unit_alpha mission_resource_token_1 intel_fragment_1)
(reinstate_path_and_activate_recon_unit recon_unit_alpha path_option_1 mission_resource_token_1)
(release_intel_from_node recon_unit_alpha intel_fragment_1)
(attach_intel_to_node assault_unit_alpha intel_fragment_1)
(consume_resource_and_recover_node assault_unit_alpha mission_resource_token_1 intel_fragment_1)
(reinstate_path_and_activate_assault_unit assault_unit_alpha path_option_2 mission_resource_token_1)
(release_intel_from_node assault_unit_alpha intel_fragment_1)
(detect_mission_node planner_agent_lead)
(assign_path_option_to_node planner_agent_lead path_option_1)
(attach_intel_to_node planner_agent_lead intel_fragment_1)
(mark_node_prepared planner_agent_lead intel_fragment_1)
(assign_primary_attachment_to_agent planner_agent_lead primary_attachment_grenade_launcher)
(attach_equipment_to_node planner_agent_lead equipment_silencer)
(install_attachment_into_loadout planner_agent_lead attachment_scope loadout_alpha)
(stage_agent_for_attachment_integration planner_agent_lead attachment_scope loadout_alpha intel_fragment_1)
(lock_agent_primary_attachment planner_agent_lead primary_attachment_grenade_launcher equipment_silencer attachment_scope loadout_alpha)
(assign_secondary_attachment_to_agent planner_agent_lead secondary_attachment_tactical_light)
(finalize_agent_secondary_attachment planner_agent_lead secondary_attachment_tactical_light attachment_scope loadout_alpha)
(finalize_agent_configuration planner_agent_lead)
(consume_resource_and_recover_node planner_agent_lead mission_resource_token_1 intel_fragment_1)
(reinstate_path_and_activate_agent planner_agent_lead path_option_1 mission_resource_token_1)
(release_intel_from_node planner_agent_lead intel_fragment_1)
(detect_mission_node planner_agent_support)
(assign_path_option_to_node planner_agent_support path_option_2)
(attach_intel_to_node planner_agent_support intel_fragment_1)
(mark_node_prepared planner_agent_support intel_fragment_1)
(unassign_primary_attachment_from_agent planner_agent_lead primary_attachment_grenade_launcher)
(assign_primary_attachment_to_agent planner_agent_support primary_attachment_grenade_launcher)
(detach_equipment_from_node planner_agent_lead equipment_silencer)
(attach_equipment_to_node planner_agent_support equipment_silencer)
(install_attachment_into_loadout planner_agent_support attachment_laser_sight loadout_alpha)
(stage_agent_for_attachment_integration planner_agent_support attachment_laser_sight loadout_alpha intel_fragment_1)
(lock_agent_primary_attachment planner_agent_support primary_attachment_grenade_launcher equipment_silencer attachment_laser_sight loadout_alpha)
(unassign_secondary_attachment_from_agent planner_agent_lead secondary_attachment_tactical_light)
(assign_secondary_attachment_to_agent planner_agent_support secondary_attachment_tactical_light)
(finalize_agent_secondary_attachment planner_agent_support secondary_attachment_tactical_light attachment_laser_sight loadout_alpha)
(finalize_agent_configuration planner_agent_support)
(consume_resource_and_recover_node planner_agent_support mission_resource_token_1 intel_fragment_1)
(reinstate_path_and_activate_agent planner_agent_support path_option_2 mission_resource_token_1)
message.reasoning:
The problem is complex, but I need to find a plan to achieve the goal: make recon_unit_alpha, assault_unit_alpha, planner_agent_lead, and planner_agent_support all entity_active.


## Assessing Activation Goals
- The task is to make recon_unit_alpha, assault_unit_alpha, planner_agent_lead, and planner_agent_support all entity_active.
- The problem involves understanding dependencies and conditions to activate each entity systematically.

## Assessing Activation Goals
- The entities recon_unit_alpha, assault_unit_alpha, planner_agent_lead, and planner_agent_support are all mission_nodes, enabling actions like detect_mission_node.
- Initially, no mission_nodes are detected, requiring detection as the first step to make them entity_active.

## Evaluating Initial State
- Approach vectors are already attached to recon_unit_alpha and assault_unit_alpha, and both planner agents have loadouts and attachments.
- Mission_marker_beta exists but isn't available initially, while mission_marker_alpha is assigned to planner_agent_lead and available.

## Tracing Activation Process
- To activate entities, they must first be entity_recovered, entity_assigned_path, and entity_has_resource, with reinstate_path_and_activate_* as the final step.
- Recovery requires deployment_ready, entity_has_intel, and mission_resource_available, forming a long chain of prerequisites.

## Tracing Activation Process  
- Detected a bottleneck with only one intel_fragment_1 and one mission_resource_token_1, suggesting entities must share resources by attaching, preparing, and releasing.  
- Paths are limited to three (path_option_1, 2, 3) for four mission_nodes, creating a challenge since all nodes require assigned paths to activate via reinstate_path_and_activate_*.  

## Investigating Resource Constraints  
- Equipment like silencer, smoke, grenade_launcher, and tactical_light are single units, likely needing cycling through entities for preparation.  
- The process involves detecting nodes, assigning paths, attaching intel, and preparing before recovery, but resource scarcity complicates activating all four nodes simultaneously.  

## Exploring Path Assignment Challenges  
- Assigning paths claims them exclusively, and reinstating doesn’t unclaim until after recovery, making it tricky with only three paths for four nodes.  
- No alternative actions found to set entity_active without assigned paths, indicating a potential issue in the initial setup for achieving the goal.

## Examining Agent and Unit Deployment  

- Agents like planner_agent_lead and planner_agent_support can achieve deployment_ready through multiple paths, including installing attachments, staging, and finalizing configurations.  
- Actions like install_attachment_into_loadout and finalize_agent_configuration require prerequisites like entity_prepared and loadout_built finalized, ensuring sequential processing due to limited resources.  

## Analyzing Resource Cycling  

- Recovery and activation, such as reinstate_path_and_activate_recon_unit, cycle paths and resources by making them available again after use, supporting sequential processing.  
- Only one intel_fragment_1, equipment, and loadout exist, requiring entities to share and release resources, with loadout_built remaining without a release mechanism.  

## Exploring Unit Readiness  

- Units like recon_unit and assault_unit become deployment_ready via mark_*_unit_ready, which requires a finalized, built loadout, limiting simultaneous readiness.  
- The process involves consuming resources and intel, with recovery needing deployment_ready and has_intel, adding to sequential dependency chains.

## Navigating Intel Sharing Challenges  
- Sharing intel, like intel_fragment_1, across units is tricky since only one exists and attaching it consumes availability.  
- Releasing intel after a unit's validation allows reattaching it to the next unit, but timing is critical to ensure each step requiring has_intel works.  

## Sequencing Unit Preparation  
- Preparing units like recon_unit_alpha involves detecting, assigning paths, attaching intel, and marking prepared, all needing has_intel at specific steps.  
- Equipment, such as silencer, is limited to one, requiring detachment from one unit, like recon, to use with another, like assault_unit_alpha, complicating the sequence.  

## Exploring Equipment and Consumable Use  
- Equipment like silencer can be attached to a unit for validation and finalization, but detaching is needed for reuse, adding steps to the process.  
- Consumables like smoke1 offer an alternative for readiness, like for assault units, when equipment is unavailable, providing flexibility in the sequence.

## Continuing Unit Preparation  
- After preparing assault_unit_alpha with intel, mark it prepared and ready with consumable smoke_grenade_1, making assault_approach_vector_alpha exhausted.  
- Confirm the assault vector, releasing smoke_grenade_1 back to available and ensuring assault_unit_alpha is configured, alongside recon_unit_alpha.  

## Assembling Loadout  
- Assemble loadout_alpha using both ready units and their confirmed vectors, consuming availability and building the loadout.  
- Finalizing loadout_alpha requires reattaching intel_fragment_1 to recon_unit_alpha after releasing it from assault_unit_alpha, ensuring prerequisites are met.  

## Marking Units for Deployment  
- Mark both recon_unit_alpha and assault_unit_alpha as deployment_ready using the finalized loadout_alpha, meeting all readiness conditions.  
- This step finalizes unit preparation, setting the stage for further activation or recovery processes.  

## Planning Resource Recovery  
- Recover recon_unit_alpha first by consuming mission_resource_token_1 and intel_fragment_1, making it recovered but leaving the resource unavailable.  
- Recovering assault_unit_alpha next requires resource availability, suggesting a sequence involving resource cycling for both units.

## Preparing Agent Equipment  
- The lead agent, planner_agent_lead, needs equipment like the silencer, currently attached to recon_unit_alpha, requiring detachment first.  
- After detaching, attach the silencer to planner_agent_lead, ensuring it’s prepared and available for locking primary attachments.  

## Locking Agent Configurations  
- Lock planner_agent_lead’s primary attachment with the grenade launcher and silencer, confirming loadout_alpha isn’t exhausted, finalizing the lock.  
- Assign and finalize a secondary attachment, tactical_light, for planner_agent_lead, marking the configuration as finalized without special abilities.  

## Activating Agent Readiness  
- Finalize planner_agent_lead’s configuration, confirming deployment readiness after consuming resources and intel, setting the stage for activation.  
- Activate planner_agent_lead using path_option_1 and mission_resource_token_1, reinstating path availability and freeing resources.  

## Preparing Second Agent  
- Detect and assign planner_agent_support to path_option_2, ensuring it’s prepared by attaching intel_fragment_1 after releasing it from planner_agent_lead.  
- Mark planner_agent_support as prepared, confirming it has loadout_alpha and an available attachment like laser_sight for further configuration.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
